Di'zok Imperial Katana  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Slashing Atk Delay: 43
DMG: 35 Dmg Bonus: 150
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +12 WIS: +5 INT: +5 MANA: +30 ENDUR: +30
SV DISEASE: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
WT: 0.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

DMG: 35 Dmg Bonus: 150

Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Di'zok blade of war
Item Type:2H Slashing
Light:Candle
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:35 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:1995

Post Comment
One schweet weap.....
QuoteReply
# Sep 28 2006 at 10:50 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592341839757
Scholar
16 posts
I got one of these as a hand-me down for my Ogre SK when I was maybe lvl 18 or so. I am now lvl 46 and the damage on this thing is incredible. On a dk blue mob I can double for 125-130 ususally (160 max). Given my STR is generally 220 or so depending on buffs/other gear. I definitely recommend this as a tanking weap!
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 01 2005 at 9:51 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) OMG!
Graphics
QuoteReply
# Sep 08 2003 at 10:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591794040064
4 posts
I quite dislike how SoE makes so many graphics mistakes. It would be almost impossible to use the blade in used as the graphic a 2hs, because the handle is only long enough for one hand.

Also, the blade used for Waki-Zashi(Correct spelling) and many Katana is in fact a Wabashi, also known as a Koshi-Gatana, or Piercing Sword.

Another thing, is that Japanese weapons were the finest ever crafted, if the stats were exact, a Fine Steel Katana would have 17/24, due to it's razor edge and selective temper.

The same issue applies to all the Bokken. Bokken were hard wood Katana, Waki-Zashi, and Tanto, thus they would be 1hb. Also, Bokken were never Bamboo, those were Shinai, a round straight practice sword, used as the first of four levels of Kendo(Shinai, Bokken, Blunt Bladed, and Live Bladed).

Edited, Mon Sep 8 23:57:40 2003
▲ Page top
Graphics
QuoteReply
# Jan 04 2007 at 8:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592267885067
42 posts
*blink*
▲ Page top
RE: Graphics
QuoteReply
# Sep 17 2005 at 6:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592260262682
285 posts
yea, and if what ur saying is true then the magic items from high end places should be 1hs around 200/30? its a game, not a museum or an atlas.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 26 2004 at 11:47 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) -------------
RE: Graphics
QuoteReply
# Mar 14 2004 at 1:02 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592253746369
8 posts
You know.....that was really great info. that being said maybe you should lighten up on the Developement team just a smidgen lol...not all of them are masters at any given form of martial arts. to most of them a katana is a sword. not different than any other and most have probably never seen them in RL. The names come from movies which have also mislead people to believe the names of different types of swords are what they are not. It's something you just have to deal with lol.
▲ Page top
RE: Graphics
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2004 at 3:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591800709177
12 posts
In all honesty, though, I think it's a fair post to make. Especially when using supposedly real equipment names such as the Japanese, Indian, Chinese, European, etc..., there should maybe be a bit more respect afforded to those cultures. Just a game? Great, use fantasy names or make up your own, like the Mithril goodies (always a good choice, gotta love that metal) or whatever... Thing is, really, if they started using just a few more original pieces instead of taking something from RL and adding stats to it, there'd be far fewer people griping about this kinda stuff and it'd have a more EQ-ish feel to it.
... This coming from a guy using Greenmist, whose graphic looks exactly the same as the wakizashis and I wouldn't want it changed at all lol...
Anyway, just my 2 cents...
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 26 2004 at 11:39 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) crimeny...i suppose yet again the supposed American developers are disrespecting the rest of the world because they arent experts in chinese history. grow up. respect is earned, not owed, and just because you aren't a chinese or indian historian doesn't mean you are a biggot (foreigners like to read "American" here). The game designers can use whatever the hell names they want to and whatever graphic they want to. Its about the game experience, not whether or not people are Loremasters of world cultures in RL. Go back to liberal acadamia and learn to be tolerant of those who chose not to study history.
RE: Graphics
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2004 at 4:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592261980472
4 posts
I thought the original post was a bit silly. It did seem like a vain attempt on the part of the author to demonstrate his arcane knowledge of ancient Japanese weapons.

That said, this response was just plain absurd. The suggestion that the author go back to 'liberal academia' identifies the writer as either a techno-barbarian or an uneducated troglodyte. I found the acidic call for tolerance particularly innane.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 21 2004 at 3:57 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) egh maube sallest should think before speaking...

Back To Looks
QuoteReply
# Nov 23 2002 at 5:31 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592253052497
Scholar
230 posts
Real lazy of Verant not use a proper Katana Graphic for this, it is more like a (ninja)To than a Katana in shape and more the size of a Wakazashi...Probably the same idiot that though ******* swords should use the same graphic as a Short sword.

/Rant off

▲ Page top
RE: Back To Looks
QuoteReply
# Jul 19 2003 at 7:12 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759821968
309 posts
Agreed. Hardly any EQ weapons look like their RL counterparts. Two-handed swords should be a lot longer, katana/wakizashi/tanto should have a curve and look like actual metal instead of straight gray sticks, scythes used for combat aren't that tall, and scimitars have thinner blades than that. The list goes on and on.

Fun Fact: Ninja-to were never really used in ancient Japan. They were a creation of Hollywood directors trying to recreate the look of a katana. Ninja used katana just like samurai, they just wielded it differently
▲ Page top
RE: Back To Looks
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2003 at 4:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591793455717
1 post
actually, you are wrong. the "ninja", usually nothing more than peasants who were payed by nobles to do dirty deeds, used a sword called a ninja-to, a blade manufactured in much the same way as european swords were, using a mold and cast instead of being hamered and folded like the katana, wakazashi, and tanto were. it wasn't a very weapon, but was sufficeint for it's job and extremely cheap and easy to make in comparison to the folded steel weapons of old japan.
▲ Page top
Great weapon
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2002 at 3:24 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592254393141
2 posts
I bought this on AB for 5K, and to me it was well worth it. The stats bonuses and weight make it truely an awesome piece to wield. When I search the bazaar I'm amazed to find the amount of nferior 2hs selling for more than this. And since I bought it, I have never seen it again. So I suppose I feel lucky, and I am definitely happy I decided to buy it.


Shadow Knight 54

Yup looks exactly like a katana, nothing really special. At level 57 hit for 200 and disced holfyorge for a crit of 575. Though the argent protector has uber stats the .5 wt of this is just insane. 0 sta drain when fightwith mass mobs, and self-hasted without any haste items able to keep taunt quite well in a main tank situation.

Demitrix

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 01 2002 at 6:38 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Sure whatever
New Chardok Weapon previous submitted 10/24/01
QuoteReply
# Nov 01 2001 at 3:07 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592252044368
1 post
I am delighted to submit the following information for your perusal and hope you find it useful. While camping Prince Selrach Di'Zok in the NEW Chardok for the enchanter epic quest, to our surprise he dropped not only the epic piece but also the Di'Zok Imperial Katana.

The Prince is a level 61mob w/about 25k hp, that can be mezzed. He has got an AoE that does considerable damage and he fluxed me w/this whirl til you hurl affect that tossed me to the ceiling and across the room 4 times. And yes this guys LOVES to summon you. I highly recommend you have MR well over 100 cause mine was 137 and I only resisted his cast about 50% of the time. His chamber contains over a dozen guards and/or golems so I recommend you try to single pull as many as you can to the outer court.

Rising Night Guild was the first guild to successfully get this new weapon on the Saryrn Server in the late hrs of October 23rd, 2001. Suzaka Raincaller was humbly awarded the privilege to be the first in the Realm of Norrath to wield its power. I dont know if this is a common drop for this boss mob or not, but I was told that OoP camped the prince the following day and the sword also dropped. This is by all stretch of the imagination an awesome weapon. My average damage output per stike now is 98-127, with the highest being 190/190 on the double attack. If I crank off a couple of stuns the damage out is unreal. Look Ma!!! Not only am I a holy knight that can take some damage, but now I can also deal 'a little bit' out. Happy hunting and I dont think you will be disappointed with this one.

Always,

-Suz

55th Season Paladin

Knights of Truth

Saryrn Server

Thank you Dinae (tactical genius) and 'Rising Nights'
▲ Page top
RE: New Chardok Weapon previous submitted 10/24/01
QuoteReply
# Apr 06 2002 at 8:40 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591776067183
9 posts
my guild took this guy out when half of the tanks were on another mob, i missed seeing the prince at all, until i saw his corpse later...we didn't need MR buffs, and we only aoe'd maybe twice. The summon part really doesn't matter unless he can gate away, OR someone tries to run. I won the roll on this great katana, and i'm hitting around 150 with it with 164str. i've only had it for one night so i can't say thats my max hit...although i'm dying to hit a crippling blow with it. Now i just need SCHW to go with it =)

Kinolyen Numair
48 Warrior
Clan of Unity
Karana Server
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 31 2001 at 5:47 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) DONG DONG, wrong...example
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 31 2001 at 5:44 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Trevelain smells like fish
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 11:47 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ( o Y o )
Di'Zok Imperial Katana
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2001 at 10:15 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592251885926
54 posts
Well, it doesnt matter who sent it in as long is it's now on the sight =)

I use this as my 2HS <Dark Elf Shadow Knight, Lvl 44> and I love it. Not only is the STR bonus GREAT for DE's, but the WEIGHT keeps us swinging almost as long as we can with Noct Blade <ie, forever>.

Common slash for me is 90-140 <146 max>, and I doubled with it on Reskin <trying it out> for 286 damage. Not bad for the boys in blue, lol <I know, that was a stupid one>.

Well, theres the skinny on it. Dont know if I was rambling or if there is any useful information in my post, just wanted to talk while at work, lol.

Have fun and, if your a Pally or Shadow Knight, get this if you can!

Trevelain Dor'Seth <Leader, Shadowstar Ascendants>
44 Shadow Knight of Innoruuk
<The Rathe>

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 9:55 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) oh the dmg bonus does change with lvl under 50. a lvl 48 war gets a 7 dmg bonus off this, but a lvl 40 war get a 6 dmg bonus off this.
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 29 2001 at 9:31 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Funny, I submitted this a WEEK ago and now it's posted under someone elses name. Makes ME feel special.
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 12:13 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Maybe some one sent in the email before you.... and he just takes a really long time to go through all his emails?
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 9:36 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I submitted 2 items to this site, and they were posted the next day... I highly doubt it's that someone else sent it in.
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 29 2001 at 9:24 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) on the top it says it has a 0 dmg bonus but on the bottom it says it has a 20 dmg bonus...... either way its uber but the picture is even ubernerer
RE: odd
QuoteReply
# Oct 29 2001 at 9:48 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The bonus is depend on the lvl that you are, if you are higher in lvl the higher the bouns.=)
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 8:08 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) That's not true. Neither is it true that if you stat a weapon you can't use, it'll say 0.
RE: odd
QuoteReply
# Nov 01 2001 at 9:42 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756133734
70 posts
I tested that with a Rusty Halberd I looted off of a decaying skeleton, at level 50 it read: DMG Bonus: 10 Then when I put it in a trade window with my best friend, a 53 rogue. He told me it said DMG Bonus: 12 So it will not always display the same thing.
▲ Page top
RE: odd
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2001 at 8:56 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765703942
34 posts
that's just in actuality, the description on it will always have the same listed dmg bonus it just doesn't mean anything until you hit lvl 50.
▲ Page top
RE: odd
QuoteReply
# Oct 31 2001 at 1:21 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246460789
View User Forum
513 posts
So you're telling me that the fact that my MotQ shows a damage bonus of 4, and my 2h-Practice Sword shows a damage bonus of 8 for my Level 40 Paladin is meaningless... right?

That's odd... 'cause I never hit for less than 15 points with the Practice Sword, since the patch.
▲ Page top
Then it just odd
QuoteReply
# Oct 31 2001 at 8:32 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1690157842670
View User Forum
Sage
814 posts
"As many of you know, two-handed weapons are not doing comparable damage to dual wielded single-handed weapons. This problem really manifests itself in the high end game. Before level 50, two-handed weapons perform fairly well. The following two-handed weapon change applies to characters level 51 and higher-"

Gordon Wrinn
Associate Producer
EverQuest Live and Expansions
Sony Online Entertainment

-------------------------------------------------

Then it is just odd. The Production Letter, and the Patch message both stated that the Two Hand Weapon change ONLY effect players over level 51.


http://boards.station.sony.com/ubb/everquest/Forum1/HTML/000340.html

*edit - added the url to message*
▲ Page top
RE: odd
QuoteReply
# Oct 31 2001 at 1:07 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965748058
76 posts
Not sure excatly what you mean here. But if you mean that the damage bonus isn't level dependant your not correct. The bonus is indeed based upon level and delay of the weapon. All level 51'st characters will have the same bonus as will all 60th, however those bonus' will be different.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 9:53 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) actully a lvl 40 war gets a 6 bonus off this while I, lvl 48 war, get a 7 bonus off this so your wrong, next time ask tank before you tell people information that is wrong.
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 9:51 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Actully, my lvl 40 war friend using the Dojo axe from Kael has a 6 dmg bonus on it. I on the other hand am a lvl 48 war and i have a 8 dmg bonus on it, so it does take effect what lvl you are you dumb pos. Next time ask a tank cause you have never used a twohander I see.
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 9:51 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Actully, my lvl 40 war friend using the Dojo axe from Kael has a 6 dmg bonus on it. I on the other hand am a lvl 48 war and i have a 8 dmg bonus on it, so it does take effect what lvl you are you dumb pos. Next time ask a tank cause you have never used a twohander I see.
